title: The digital welcome book your guests will actually use.
subtitle: Mobile guide + printable PDF. Everything in one link. In 3 languages.
body: No apps. No PDFs lost in email threads. Just a clean, beautiful guide your guests open the moment they arrive.
caption: First impressions matter — and the welcome message you send your guests is part of that. MyGuest gives every guest a beautiful, easy-to-use guide from the moment they book. One link. All the info they need. No more 11pm messages asking for the WiFi password.
notes: Intro post — use as first post or pinned to profile.

title: Everything your guests need, right on their phone.
subtitle: No app downloads. No lost PDFs. Just one clean link that works.
caption: Your guests are arriving tired. They don't want to search through emails for check-in instructions. They just want things to be easy. That's exactly what MyGuest is built for — everything in one place, beautiful and simple on any phone.
notes: Core brand statement. Keep the quote mark large and impactful.

title: A welcome book as beautiful as your property.
subtitle: 4 design styles. 3 languages. One link to share.
body: Your property has a personality. Your guest guide should too. Choose from 4 thoughtfully designed styles that match your space.
caption: We believe your welcome guide should feel as considered as the space itself. That's why MyGuest comes in 4 design styles — Coastal, Minimalist, Sunset, and Classic — each crafted to match the mood of your property. Because details matter.
notes: Focus on design quality. Good for design-conscious hosts.

title: What's included.
subtitle: Everything your guests need. Nothing they don't.
caption: A lot goes into a great guest experience — and a great guest guide covers all of it. Check-in details, house rules, local recommendations, emergency contacts. Mobile and print, in three languages. All in one link you share with every booking.
notes: Functional, informative post. Great for hosts comparing options.

title: Three languages. One guide. Happy guests.
subtitle: English, Spanish, and French — all included at no extra cost.
caption: Your guests come from all over the world. Now your welcome book does too. Every MyGuest guide is available in English, Spanish, and French — automatically. No extra work on your end, just a better experience for every guest who walks through your door.
notes: Highlight the multilingual feature specifically.

title: Mobile guide + printable PDF.
subtitle: Because some guests prefer paper — and that's perfectly fine.
body: Leave a beautifully printed copy on the table. Share the link with the booking. Both formats, one guide.
caption: Some guests scroll their phones. Others like a printed page on the kitchen table. With MyGuest, you get both — a mobile-friendly web guide and a beautifully formatted printable PDF, ready to leave for every arrival. One purchase, two formats.
notes: Focus on the dual-format (mobile + PDF) feature.

title: Your guests are messaging you at 11pm. Again.
body: Check-in questions. WiFi passwords. Where's the nearest pharmacy? You've answered all of these dozens of times — and you'll answer them again with the next guest.
caption: If you've ever replied to a guest message while half-asleep, this one's for you. The same questions, booking after booking. It doesn't have to be this way. A clear, beautiful welcome guide — shared before arrival — answers most questions before they're even asked.
notes: Pain point post — high engagement potential. Relatable tone.

title: Where's the WiFi? Again.
body: You put it in the email. You mentioned it in the booking confirmation. And somehow, they still ask. Every. Single. Guest.
caption: WiFi is the #1 question every host gets, no matter how many places they put it. With MyGuest, your guide is the first thing guests open when they arrive — and the WiFi password is right there, easy to find, without digging through emails.
notes: Specific pain point — WiFi. Very relatable.

title: Before MyGuest vs. After
caption: The difference isn't just visual — it's the whole experience. Before: scattered PDFs, repeated questions, guests who can't find anything. After: one clean link, three languages, happy guests who feel taken care of from the moment they arrive.
notes: Before/after comparison — good for saves and shares.

title: What guests see vs. what they should see
caption: A PDF buried in an email thread is not a welcome. A beautiful, mobile-friendly guide that loads instantly — that's a welcome. Small things make a big difference to how guests feel about their stay, and how they write their reviews.
notes: Second before/after — focus on reviews and impressions.

title: One link. Everything they need.
benefits: Mobile-friendly guide guests can open instantly
Printable PDF for guests who prefer paper
caption: You don't need a tech degree to give guests a great experience. MyGuest is one link you share with every booking — and inside, they find everything: check-in details, house rules, local tips, and more. Clean, calm, and beautiful on any phone.
notes: Primary solution statement on dark teal background.

title: Fewer questions. Better reviews.
benefits: Answers check-in questions before they're asked
Guests feel informed and cared for
caption: When guests feel informed and welcome, they're more relaxed — and that shows in their reviews. A beautiful welcome guide is one of the simplest things you can do to improve your rating and reduce the back-and-forth that eats your time.
notes: Focus on 5-star reviews and reduced host stress.

title: Built for hosts who care about the details.
benefits: Every detail of your property, organized
Personalised to your space and brand
caption: You've put real thought into your space. Your welcome guide should reflect that same care. MyGuest helps you present every detail — check-in, amenities, local recommendations — in a format that feels as considered as the property itself.
notes: Targeting design-conscious hosts.

title: How many times have you sent the check-in instructions?
body: They were in the booking confirmation. Then in the follow-up message. Then again the morning of check-in. And still they couldn't find them.
caption: The irony of check-in information is that the more places you put it, the harder it is to find. One clear, beautiful guide — always at the same link — is worth a hundred messages. That's the idea behind MyGuest.
notes: Third pain point post — checkin instructions frustration.

title: Guests who don't read your messages... until it's too late.
body: You wrote a thorough welcome message. It had everything. Parking, check-in code, house rules. They opened the door and still called you asking where to park.
caption: Long messages don't get read — especially when guests are tired and just want to drop their bags. A short link to a beautiful, easy guide is what actually gets opened. MyGuest is designed to be the thing guests open when they arrive, not the email they skip.
notes: Empathetic pain point — guests not reading long messages.

title: How it works.
subtitle: Four simple steps. No tech skills needed.
caption: Getting your MyGuest guide set up is genuinely simple. You pay once, fill in a form with your property details, and we send your guide to your inbox. From there, you share the link with every booking — and your guests have everything they need. That's it.
notes: Process explanation — good for new followers discovering MyGuest.

title: From payment to published guide: 48 hours.
subtitle: We do the work. You share the link.
caption: Most hosts are surprised by how fast it is. You fill in a form with your details, and within 48 hours your personalized guide is in your inbox — mobile-ready, trilingual, and beautiful. After that, one link is all you need for every booking.
notes: Emphasize the speed and simplicity of the process.

title: One-time setup. Ongoing results.
subtitle: Set it up once — use it for every guest, forever.
caption: The best thing about a good welcome guide is that you make it once and it keeps working. Every new guest gets the same great first impression, the same clear information, the same professional welcome — without any extra effort from you.
notes: Highlight the one-time setup, evergreen value.

title: No tech skills needed.
subtitle: Just answer the form — we handle the rest.
caption: You don't need to know how to build a website or design a PDF. You just fill in a simple form with your property details — check-in info, house rules, local tips — and we turn it into a beautiful, branded guide. You focus on hosting. We handle the rest.
notes: Targeting tech-hesitant hosts.

title: Fresh, clean, coastal.
subtitle: The Coastal style is made for properties near the water.
body: Light, airy, fresh. Blues and teals that feel like sea and sky. Perfect for beach houses, lake homes, and coastal retreats.
caption: If your property is by the sea, the lake, or anywhere with a view of water — the Coastal style was made for you. Fresh teal tones, clean typography, and an airy layout that feels exactly right. See the live demo at myguestguide.com.
notes: Template showcase — Coastal style.

title: Pure, restrained, perfect.
subtitle: The Minimalist style for hosts who believe less is more.
body: Pure, restrained, sophisticated. Neutral tones and maximum white space. For design-conscious hosts who prefer less.
caption: For hosts who believe that good design is invisible — the Minimalist style. Warm neutrals, generous white space, and quiet elegance. No clutter, just clarity. Your guests will notice the difference, even if they can't say exactly why.
notes: Template showcase — Minimalist style.

title: Warm, golden, inviting.
subtitle: The Sunset style for properties that feel like golden hour.
body: Warm, golden, inviting. Coral and terracotta hues that evoke golden hour. Great for city apartments and desert homes.
caption: Coral, terracotta, golden hour. The Sunset style brings warmth and richness to your welcome guide. Perfect for city apartments, desert retreats, or any property with that warm Mediterranean or South American feel. Visit myguestguide.com to see the full demo.
notes: Template showcase — Sunset style.

title: Timeless, elegant, refined.
subtitle: The Classic style for properties that value tradition.
body: Timeless, elegant, refined. Deep greens and warm neutrals for properties that prize sophistication and tradition.
caption: Some properties have a story. A character. The Classic style honours that — with deep greens, warm neutrals, and an editorial layout that feels both timeless and sophisticated. For chalets, historic flats, and properties with personality.
notes: Template showcase — Classic style.

title: Your digital welcome book, ready to share.
caption: For the price of a nice dinner, your guests get a beautiful, professional welcome guide — and you get fewer questions, happier guests, and better reviews. $29. One-time. No subscription. myguestguide.com
notes: Price CTA — first sales post.

title: Stop sending the same message to every guest.
caption: Every booking, you copy-paste the same check-in instructions. Every arrival, you answer the same three questions. For $29, you can have a beautiful, personalized guide that does all of that for you — automatically, with every guest who gets your link.
notes: Price CTA — focus on time saved.

title: Upgrade your guest experience today.
caption: The launch price won't last. For $29, you get a complete digital welcome book — mobile guide, printable PDF, three languages, your style. One-time payment, yours forever. See what's included at myguestguide.com.
notes: Price CTA — urgency without pressure.

title: Make your property unforgettable from the first message.
caption: The welcome you give a guest shapes everything that follows — how they feel in the space, how they treat it, how they talk about it in their review. A beautiful, clear welcome guide is the simplest investment you can make in your hosting experience.
notes: Price CTA — emotional, about the full guest experience.

title: Your guests deserve a great arrival.
benefits: Beautiful guide that opens instantly on any phone
All your details organized and easy to find
3 languages so no guest feels lost
caption: Arrival day is the moment that sets the tone for the whole stay. When guests can find everything easily — the code, the rules, the local tips — they feel welcomed. That feeling carries through to the review they leave at the end.
notes: Solution post focused on the arrival experience.

title: The welcome your property deserves.
subtitle: Professional, warm, and completely yours.
caption: You've invested in your property. The furniture, the lighting, the little details that make it feel special. Your welcome guide should match that same level of care. MyGuest is that guide — thoughtful, beautiful, and personalized to your space.
notes: Brand/quality post — late in sequence.

title: Every guest. One link. A better stay.
subtitle: Join the hosts who've already made the switch.
body: Beautiful, multilingual, mobile-ready. Your digital welcome book, done.
caption: This is what we believe: every guest deserves to arrive informed, welcomed, and at ease. And every host deserves to spend less time answering the same questions. MyGuest makes both of those things possible — with one beautiful guide, shared once, working forever. Visit myguestguide.com.
notes: Closing/evergreen post. Works as a fresh intro or recap.
